title: rubus caesius subsp. leucophaeus
intro: Rubus caesius subsp. leucophaeus, commonly known as the white-fruited blackberry or white blackberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its edible white fruits. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming Rubus caesius subsp. leucophaeus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing a rich source of vitamins and minerals. The herbal preparations of Rubus caesius subsp. leucophaeus include jams, preserves, and teas, which can be made from the fruits, leaves, and roots of the plant.
